section.title: Meetings and recording
section.intro: AI meeting tools now record through laptop microphones with no visible indicator. Tools, consent requirements, and what organizations need to decide.

resources.title: ChatGPT Record Mode
resources.author: OpenAI
resources.description: Native meeting recording through the ChatGPT app. Records ambient audio via device microphone with no bot joining and no notification to other participants.

resources.title: Granola
resources.author: granola.ai
resources.description: Meeting transcription from laptop microphone with no bot joining and no participant notification. Includes reusable templates, cross-meeting search, and an enterprise notification feature (pilot January 2026) that alerts other participants.

resources.title: Guidance on Using AI Notetakers During Meetings
resources.author: Fordham University
resources.description: Institutional policy addressing consent, data retention, and meeting types where AI notetakers should not be used. Usable as a template.

resources.title: Ambient Listening AI in Clinical Settings
resources.author: AMA Journal of Ethics
resources.description: Research on how patients alter behavior when AI records clinical encounters. Participants spoke differently, disclosed less, and filtered sensitive information.

resources.title: NYC Bar Formal Opinion 2025-6: AI Recording Ethics
resources.author: New York City Bar Association
resources.description: Ethics opinion on AI recording with clients. Establishes that professional ethics require disclosure of AI recording regardless of whether recording is technically legal.

resources.title: AI Meeting Recording: Multi-Jurisdiction Consent
resources.author: Reed Smith LLP
resources.description: In multi-jurisdiction calls, the most restrictive jurisdiction's consent rules apply. One all-party-consent participant triggers recording consent requirements for the entire meeting.

resources.title: Overcoming Organizational Barriers to AI Adoption
resources.author: Harvard Business Review
resources.description: Organizations that center employees in AI adoption decisions are 7x more effective than those that mandate top-down.

resources.title: AI Adoption Confidence Collapsing
resources.author: Fortune
resources.description: Trust in company generative AI programs fell 31% in three months. Organizations that deploy AI tools without employee input see sharper confidence drops.

resources.title: SACHRP Recommendations on AI in Human Subjects Research
resources.author: OHRP/SACHRP
resources.description: Advisory committee recommendations on how AI tools intersect with human subjects protections.
resources.note: SACHRP was terminated in March 2025 as part of HHS restructuring (82,000 to 62,000 staff). The recommendations remain substantively relevant, but the institutional mechanism for updating federal AI guidance in research is gone.

resources.title: AI Meeting Recording: Legal Risks and Wiretapping Laws
resources.author: JD Supra
resources.description: Recording laws vary by jurisdiction. In many US states and most non-US jurisdictions, all parties must consent. Using an AI notetaker without disclosure creates legal liability.

resources.title: Microsoft Copilot in Teams
resources.author: Microsoft
resources.description: For organizations using Microsoft 365, Copilot is built into Teams. Data stays within the tenant.

warningCallout.title: The culture gap
warningCallout.body: Many organizations ban meeting bots but haven't addressed tools that record through laptop microphones. ChatGPT's Record Mode, Granola, and similar tools capture ambient audio with no bot joining and no notification to other participants. Organizations that handle confidential partner relationships and sensitive population data need a stated position on ambient recording.
